Buttons: Know It, Still Learning, Skip. Progress bar during study session. Results screen: cards mastered vs needs review. Edit deck: add/edit/delete cards. Create new deck sheet with name and color. Import cards from text. Persist with Codable + UserDefaults.

Build me an app where I can do push-ups and then my app will count those push-ups. I don't know if augmented reality would be a technology that we need to add here. It basically needs to use a video camera, maybe the user's phone most likely, or plug-ins or whatever. It would basically just use that user's phone. Then, every push-up they do, it would detect each completed push-up, list those on the screen, and then eventually the user could just go back to the app and hit complete or something. It could then log how many push-ups they've done, maybe a day by day thing.

I'm wondering; I feel like we should have a flip view camera, because maybe if there's a selfie view, if the user wants to do more of a selfie view, to ensure that they can see what it's capturing. I feel like that would be the really important camera view. Build this.

userNotes: I hit the start workout button within the app, and it kind of just freezes. I then checked the Xcode logs, and this is what I'm seeing. This is why I'm giving the function a two, mainly because it's broken. I would assume it maybe has potential, but it's just not.  It's the whole core of the app. It can open a camera and then tell the user how many pushups they've done, so it's a pretty big deal if it's not going to work. The app issue is -- This app has crashed because it attempted to access privacy-sensitive data without a usage description.  The app's Info.plist must contain an NSCameraUsageDescription key with a string value explaining to the user how the app uses this data.
prompt:
Create an app where I can do push ups and then my app will count each push up that I do. I don't know if augmented reality is needed for this, but if it is, then definitely implement it. Basically, we'll just look at my push ups and then count them as I complete them. It'll then display them live on the screen, more so like a FaceTime appearance. Mostly video, but maybe just a number of push ups. It's mostly a camera view, but then a number on the screen. The main focus would be the camera view.

It should be counting push ups as I'm doing them, and then it'll also have a way so that I can stop the workout. It would just give me a screen that would summarize how many push ups I've done. You can be creative. Maybe it would tell me how many calories I've burned by using the number of push ups and then running some formula. It should maybe have a way to look at the previous days, kind of like a log, but you can use more formal terminology for the user. More like a recap of different days or by week or something. Build this.

Mostly video, but maybe just a number of push ups. It's mostly a camera view, but then a number on the screen. The main focus would be the camera view. It should be counting push ups as I'm doing them, and then it'll also have a way so that I can stop the workout. It would just give me a screen that would summarize how many push ups I've done. You can be creative. Maybe it would tell me how many calories I've burned by using the number of push ups and then running some formula. It should maybe have a way to look at the previous days, kind of like a log, but you can use more formal terminology for the user. More like a recap of different days or by week or something. Build this.

Build a camera placeholder. One screen: button “Take photo”; tap shows alert “Camera not configured.” (No real camera for MVP.)
Build an in-app Safari screen. One screen: SFSafariViewController or WKWebView loading https://apple.com and a Close button.

Build a 5-screen onboarding with progress. Horizontal paging with page indicator dots and progress bar. Each page: illustration placeholder, title, subtitle. Page 3: permission prompt (notifications toggle). Page 4: preference picker (choose interests from a grid). Last page: Get Started and Skip buttons. Animated transitions between pages. Remember completion state in UserDefaults so onboarding only shows once.

Build a todo app with categories. Main screen: segmented control to filter (All, Active, Completed). List of tasks with checkboxes, due dates, and priority colors (high=red, medium=orange, low=green). Add task sheet: title, due date picker, priority picker, optional notes. Swipe to delete. Sort by due date or priority. Persist with Codable + UserDefaults. Show completion percentage at top.

Build a todo app with categories. Main screen: segmented control to filter (All, Active, Completed). List of tasks with checkboxes, due dates, and priority colors (high=red, medium=orange, low=green). Add task sheet: title, due date picker, priority picker, optional notes. Swipe to delete. Sort by due date or priority. Persist with Codable + UserDefaults. Show completion percentage at top.

Build an expense tracker with categories. Main screen: list of expenses (amount, label, category icon, date) with running total at top. Add expense sheet: amount field, label, category picker (Food, Transport, Entertainment, etc.), date picker. Filter by category using segmented control. Monthly summary view showing total per category. Persist with Codable + UserDefaults.

Build a Pomodoro timer with session tracking. Main screen: circular timer with work/break phases. Configurable: work duration (15–60 min), short break (3–10 min), long break (15–30 min), sessions before long break. Start/Pause/Skip controls. Session counter (e.g. 3/4). Today’s stats: completed pomodoros, total focus time. History tab: daily summaries for past 7 days. Persist with Codable + UserDefaults.

Build a 3-tab app with proper navigation. Tab 1 (Home): dashboard with summary cards. Tab 2 (Browse): searchable list with NavigationStack to detail screens. Tab 3 (Profile): profile view with settings. Badge on Browse tab showing item count. Proper back navigation within each tab. Consistent styling across tabs. Persist minimal state with UserDefaults.

Build a personal finance dashboard with Swift Charts. One screen: a bar chart showing income vs spending for the last 7 days (use sample data like 3 income and 4 spending values). Below the chart, a list of recent transactions (title and amount, positive green and negative red). At the top, a savings goal progress view (e.g. "$200 / $1000"). Use SwiftUI Chart for the bar chart. Keep data in memory or a simple model.

Buttons: Know It, Still Learning, Skip. Progress bar during study session. Results screen: cards mastered vs needs review. Edit deck: add/edit/delete cards. Create new deck sheet with name and color. Import cards from text. Persist with Codable + UserDefaults.

Build a 5-screen onboarding with progress. Horizontal paging with page indicator dots and progress bar. Each page: illustration placeholder, title, subtitle. Page 3: permission prompt (notifications toggle). Page 4: preference picker (choose interests from a grid). Last page: Get Started and Skip buttons. Animated transitions between pages. Remember completion state in UserDefaults so onboarding only shows once.

Build a camera app placeholder. Main screen: viewfinder area (dark rectangle) with capture button, flash toggle, and camera flip button. Gallery strip at bottom showing last 3 captures (colored placeholders). Capture creates a new placeholder with timestamp. Gallery screen: grid of all captures with date sections. Detail view: full-screen with share, delete, and info (date, size placeholder). Filters preview strip (Original, B&W, Sepia, Vivid).

Build a habit tracker with Liquid Glass design. One screen: a list of habits (e.g. Exercise, Read, Meditate). Each habit is a card showing its name, current streak count, and a "Check in" button. Use iOS 26 and .glassEffect() on the cards. When the user taps Check in, animate the update and increment the streak. Store streaks in memory or UserDefaults. Include an option to add a new habit.
Build a dice roller app. One screen: a single die icon and a Roll button. Show result 1–6. Optional: second die and sum. Fun, minimal.

An app where you describe what kind of music you’re feeling and it pairs with Apple Music library to give you a curated playlist

It would maybe ask 2-3 critical questions like how many minutes do you have, so it can figure out how many songs to add in the playlist

Build me an app where it tracks my pushups, it would use the phones camera and it needs to have the default be the selfie mode, with a way for the user to flip cameras from the front camera to the back camera. it should have permissions implemented if needed, like it maybe says allow app to use camera, if that's necessary. It should mainly be a camera screen like facetime, but have a number of pushups number that's easy to read from say 6-8 feet away while i'm doing the pushups. the counter needs to update live and it should be looking at my motion to detect when a pushup is done. be creative as well and add in a few more screens that would be useful for the user. maybe one screen shows the history of how many pushups they did by day, maybe goals, how close they are to hitting their goals like a little bar thing that shows how much was completed. like if a user wants 20 pushups in a day and they did 15 that day, it would fill the par 75% to reflect this.
test

Build me an app where it tracks my pushups, it would use the phones camera and it needs to have the default be the selfie mode, with a way for the user to flip cameras from the front camera to the back camera. it should have permissions implemented if needed, like it maybe says allow app to use camera, if that's necessary. It should mainly be a camera screen like facetime, but have a number of pushups number that's easy to read from say 6-8 feet away while i'm doing the pushups. the counter needs to update live and it should be looking at my motion to detect when a pushup is done. be creative as well and add in a few more screens that would be useful for the user. maybe one screen shows the history of how many pushups they did by day, maybe goals, how close they are to hitting their goals like a little bar thing that shows how much was completed. like if a user wants 20 pushups in a day and they did 15 that day, it would fill the par 75% to reflect this.

I am now seeing where it'll say "body detected" or "body not detected". That's a nice little improvement, but I am doing the push-ups and it's not counting. We need to figure out what needs to happen so that, when I do a push-up, the app is then detecting that and then pretty much counting live on the screen. You don't have to change any of the buttons or anything really. Keep the design and everything the same. I just need you to improve that functionality so we can get it to count the push-ups.

Build me an app where it tracks my pushups, it would use the phones camera and it needs to have the default be the selfie mode, with a way for the user to flip cameras from the front camera to the back camera. it should have permissions implemented if needed, like it maybe says allow app to use camera, if that's necessary. It should mainly be a camera screen like facetime, but have a number of pushups number that's easy to read from say 6-8 feet away while i'm doing the pushups. the counter needs to update live and it should be looking at my motion to detect when a pushup is done. be creative as well and add in a few more screens that would be useful for the user. maybe one screen shows the history of how many pushups they did by day, maybe goals, how close they are to hitting their goals like a little bar thing that shows how much was completed. like if a user wants 20 pushups in a day and they did 15 that day, it would fill the par 75% to reflect this.

The pushup counter is double-counting -- it counts 2 for every 1 pushup. Fix the rep counting so one full down-up cycle equals exactly one rep. Use a 2-state machine (idle and wentDown), a smoothing alpha of 0.2, a cooldown of at least 1.0 second, and a hysteresis gap of at least 0.25 between the down threshold (0.60) and up threshold (0.35)."

It should mainly be a camera screen like facetime, but have a number of pushups number that's easy to read from say 6-8 feet away while i'm doing the pushups. the counter needs to update live and it should be looking at my motion to detect when a pushup is done. be creative as well and add in a few more screens that would be useful for the user. maybe one screen shows the history of how many pushups they did by day, maybe goals, how close they are to hitting their goals like a little bar thing that shows how much was completed. like if a user wants 20 pushups in a day and they did 15 that day, it would fill the par 75% to reflect this.

Couples Receipts

Build me an app where it logs different things for a couple. It would basically have an option where a user can upload a receipt and then our app can look at the receipt and itemize it digitally. For example if there is a loaf of bread on the receipt, it will get the entire wording of the item and then it should let the user say whose item it was. If they want to do a certain split percentage-wise, there should be a way for a couple to share access, like a dual account. Somehow we would need a good way to implement that, maybe a partner code or some code that connects the accounts.

I would also need to have a summary of who owes who what. For example if person A is owed $100 and person B is owed $20, it would figure out the net amount so it is just one easy number. There also needs to be a payments tab so each payment would then instantly affect the balance. For example if person A is owed $80 and person B makes the payment of $80, the balance needs to reflect that so it would be a zero balance. 

I also want this to be added as well. Basically there should be another section where if person A owes person B an amount of money (we'll say $1,000) it should log that amount and there should be a separate payments thing for this. Specifically it would be separate from the day-to-day transactions. The day-to-day transactions might be:
* groceries
* gas
* restaurants
* things that one person in the couple might pay for the other person and then they need reimbursement
Whereas there's more of a debt section. I don't know if that's going to be the right name for this second section but we'll just say debts for now. The debt section should have a master balance and then its own unique payments thing as well. If a user makes a payment it's only going to touch the payments for this debt section. That payment would not touch the amount owed on the other component if that makes sense.

projectName: Polished Barcode And QR Code Scanner
prompt: Detect barcodes/QRs from images (still frames) rather than live metadata scanning.

projectName: Polished Barcode And QR Code Scanner
prompt: Update barcode decoding so it shows BOTH raw and normalized values. Add carrier-aware normalization candidates: UPS 1Z (first 18), USPS 22 digits, FedEx 12/15/20 digits, plus suffix candidates (last 22/20/15/12) when the payload has extra prefixes. Copy should default to the normalized primary value, with an option to copy raw.
